commit.message:
ci(release): add root trusted publishing pipeline (#11)

Add root Release Please state for the MartiX.Dotnet.Templates pack and wire the package version to version.txt so release automation owns the published version.

Also add the repository-level release workflow with native OIDC-based NuGet trusted publishing and clarify the boundary between root package publishing and generated-repo release assets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: redesign practice modals, fix alignments, forum filters, module cards

- Remove duplicate sleep module phase 0 exercise page
- Create PracticeModalShell component with flashcard-inspired design (6 color themes, atmospheric orbs, glassmorphic header)
- Migrate all 145 practice modal files to use PracticeModalShell
- Fix sticky header alignment in Search, Forum, and Dashboard (px moved to inner max-w-md container)
- Redesign forum filter buttons as pill-style (All / My Discussions / Filters)
- Add missing forum translation keys: myDiscussions, filters, noOwnDiscussions, noOwnDiscussionsHint
- Remove 'YOUR PATH' section from ModulesPage (keep Resume Reading + All Modules)
- Redesign Dashboard 'Your path' section with horizontal scrolling module cards using illustrations
- Remove bookmark/pin button from all 10 module landing pages (View components)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: DA1/DA2 クエリの送信を根本的に抑止する (#103)

* fix: DA1/DA2 クエリの送信を根本的に抑止する

【新たに判明した問題】
「61;4;6;7;14;21;22;23;24;28;32;42;52c」(DA1 応答)・
「0;10;1c」(DA2 応答) が stdin に混入していた。
これは vim が DA2 クエリ (\e[>c) を送信していたことが原因。

【変更内容】
- dot_vimrc: t_RV・t_RB・t_RF を空に設定し vim のクエリを全て無効化
  - t_RV=: DA2（バージョン問い合わせ）クエリを送信させない
  - t_RB=: OSC 11（背景色）クエリを送信させない（set background=dark と二重対策）
  - t_RF=: OSC 10（前景色）クエリを送信させない
- dot_tmux.conf: default-terminal を tmux-256color に切り替え（条件付き）
  - tmux-256color は terminfo が完全で、プログラムが DA クエリで機能補完する動作を抑制
  - infocmp で存在確認後に切り替え、利用不可の場合は screen-256color にフォールバック
- dot_zshrc / dot_bashrc: ドレイン関数を2フェーズに改善
  - フェーズ1: 非ブロッキック即時チェック（データなし時は遅延ゼロ）
  - フェーズ2: データがあった場合のみ20ms待機ループで残りも全て捕捉
  - 起動時ドレインを末尾に追加（全ツールロード後に100ms待機して応答を破棄）

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: terminal-overrides Tc を terminal-features RGB に置き換える

【変更理由】
terminal-overrides の Tc 設定は tmux がアタッチ時に外部ターミナルへ
OSC 10/11 クエリを送信する原因となっていた。
応答 (`\e]11;rgb:xxxx/xxxx/xxxx\a`) がタイミングによってシェルの
stdin に漏れ込み `^[]11;rgb:0c0c/0c0c/0c0c^[` として表示されていた。

terminal-features の RGB は宣言型（クエリなし）でサポートを有効化するため
アタッチ時の OSC 10/11 クエリを回避できる（tmux 3.2+ 対応）。

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: 起動時ドレインを opt-in 化しユーザー先行入力の破棄を防止する

【変更内容】
- dot_bashrc / dot_zshrc の起動時ドレインをデフォルト無効に変更
  - bash: BASH_DRAIN_ESC_ON_STARTUP=1 を設定した場合のみ実行
  - zsh: ZSH_STARTUP_ESC_DRAIN を設定した場合のみ実行
- 起動時ドレインを -t 0（非ブロッキング）に変更
  - 以前の -t 0.1（100ms 待機）はユーザーの先行入力を破棄する恐れがあった
- コメントを「二層防衛」の役割分担として更新
  - PROMPT_COMMAND / precmd ドレインが主要手段
  - 起動時ドレインは opt-in の安全網

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: akubiusa <222856114+akubiusa@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

MCP OAuth: dynamic client registration, crash fix

- Discovery follows MCP protected resource spec + registration endpoint
- Registration-provided clientId/redirect_uris override CLI config
- Cloud-build auth working end-to-end (register -> discover -> OAuth -> token)
- Token injection checks Caco auth store before CLI tokens
- Fix crash: track active temp server, close before starting new one
- Error handler on server.listen prevents EADDRINUSE crash

feat: enhance TallerPage and ProyectosPage with new features

TallerPage.vue:
- Add 'New Ticket' creation modal with full equipment intake form
  (brand, model, serial, accessories, client ID, technician, priority,
  description, client email)
- Add 'Work Order' modal for printing/sharing ticket details with
  tracking link and signature fields
- Add tracking link copy button in ticket detail view
- Display reparacion details (marca, modelo, serial) in detail modal

ProyectosPage.vue:
- Add priority field with color indicators on kanban task cards
- Add assignee field (id_asignado) with user name display
- Add due date field (fecha_vencimiento) with overdue highlighting
- Add labels/tags support (etiquetas) with JSON array storage
- Add task detail modal with full task information
- Load usuarios store for assignee selection dropdown
- Add fecha_inicio/fecha_fin fields to project creation form
- Convert comma-separated tags to JSON array before sending to API
- Better dark mode support across all components

OrdenTrabajoPage.vue:
- Remove unused 'Package' import (pre-existing TS error fix)

commit.message:
Add RF/SATCOM and Launch Planner calculator tabs

- Add SATCOM tab with link budget, station comparison, route comparison,
  interference/jamming, and SIGINT sub-tabs
- Add Launch Planner tab with launch-to-orbit, orbit transfer, phasing/RPO,
  lunar transfer, and delta-V budget sub-tabs
- Add feature cards on home tab for new tabs
- Wire all calculation functions with proper module imports
- Add acceptance tests for LinkBudget, LaunchSites, and LunarTransfer

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix tray hide/show lifecycle and timer resync

Route close behavior through a shared hide-to-tray path, disable default hide-on-close to rely on BeforeClose interception, remove the risky native Cmd+Q monitor path, make tray show/quit callbacks async for stability, and resync frontend timer UI after tray stop/restore.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add 12 mission-planning ES modules

New modules for launch planning, transfer design, phasing/RPO,
delta-V budgeting, mission sequencing, lunar transfer, and stubs
for Lambert solver, porkchop plots, and interplanetary transfers.

- js/launch-sites.js: Launch site data, azimuth, rotation benefit
- js/launch-vehicles.js: Vehicle classes, suitability, insertion dV
- js/launch-planner.js: Launch-to-orbit feasibility calculator
- js/window-search.js: Generic window search engine
- js/transfer-planner.js: Hohmann, bi-elliptic, combined transfers
- js/phasing.js: Phasing orbits and simplified RPO planning
- js/delta-v-budget.js: Mission delta-V budget builder
- js/mission-sequencer.js: Mission leg sequencing and timeline
- js/lunar-transfer.js: Earth-to-Moon transfer planning
- js/lambert.js: Lambert solver stub
- js/porkchop.js: Porkchop plot stub
- js/interplanetary.js: Interplanetary transfer stub

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Add dark body styles to style.css

commit.message:
fix: replace html2canvas with native Canvas API for share card

- html2canvas was unreliable on mobile prod (tainted canvas, hanging)
- Native Canvas API draws card pixel-by-pixel - zero third-party issues
- Image loaded via crossOrigin='anonymous' with 6s timeout
- Pre-generates PNG on mount, navigator.share() fires instantly on tap
- Keeps HTML preview for visual display, Canvas only for export

commit.message:
ci: remove continue-on-error to enforce strict CI checks

commit.message:
Add 10 satcom ES module files: RF constants, link budget, interference, quality, network routing, ground stations, antennas, SIGINT, optimizer

New modules for satellite communications analysis:
- rf-constants.js: RF bands, modulation presets, Boltzmann constant
- atmosphere.js: Weather presets, atmospheric loss, scintillation
- link-budget.js: FSPL, single-hop and uplink/downlink budget engine
- interference.js: Jamming analysis and mitigation comparison
- quality.js: Service quality classification and mission impact
- satcom-network.js: Route leg/composition/comparison
- groundstations.js: Station loading, evaluation, and ranking
- antennas.js: Antenna presets and parametric models
- sigint.js: SIGINT opportunity assessment (educational model)
- optimizer.js: Weighted scoring and candidate ranking framework

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement testing infrastructure, Docker setup, and CI/CD pipeline (#2)

* feat: implement testing infrastructure and Docker containerization (Phases 1-2)

Phase 1: Testing Infrastructure
- Add Vitest testing framework with 6 passing tests across 3 components
- Configure Vitest with jsdom environment and global test utilities
- Create test files for Navbar, CountdownTimer, and FileUpload components
- Add test coverage reporting with @vitest/coverage-v8
- Set up @testing-library/react for component testing
- Add test scripts to package.json (test, test:run, test:ui, coverage)
- Configure TypeScript with Vitest globals support
- Add coverage/ and .vitest-cache/ to .gitignore

Phase 2: Docker Multi-Stage Build
- Create optimized multi-stage Dockerfile (20.6MB final image)
  * Stage 1 (deps): Install production dependencies
  * Stage 2 (builder): Build Vite application with build args
  * Stage 3 (runner): Serve with nginx:1.27-alpine-slim
- Add docker-compose.yaml for local development workflow
- Configure nginx.conf with SPA routing, gzip, and caching headers
- Create .dockerignore to optimize build context
- Add .env.example template for environment variables
- Move build-time dependencies to devDependencies (tailwindcss, @tailwindcss/vite)

Documentation & Configuration
- Add AGENTS.md with comprehensive developer guidelines
- Create docker-cicd-learning-plan.md (4-phase learning roadmap)
- Add phase-1-testing-implementation.md detailed plan
- Create guidelines.md for AI agent context
- Update TODO.md with completed tasks

Features & Fixes
- Add storage.ts utility for Supabase file operations
- Update gallery.tsx with file upload functionality
- Enhance comp-547.tsx component
- Update App.tsx with improved routing

All tests passing (6/6). Docker image optimized to 20.6MB.
Ready for Phase 3: GitHub Actions CI/CD implementation.

* feat: implement comprehensive GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline (Phase 3)

- Add test workflow with Node.js matrix testing (20/22/24)
- Add Docker workflow with multi-stage build and GHCR publishing
- Add smoke test workflow for deployment verification
- Update README with CI/CD badges, workflow documentation, and semantic versioning guide
- Rename docker-compose.yaml to docker-compose.yml for consistency
- Add Phase 3 implementation plan documentation

Implements automated testing, containerization, and continuous deployment with semantic versioning support. Total pipeline runtime ~5-6 minutes with parallel execution.

* fix: correct typo in Docker registry URL (ghrcr.io -> ghcr.io)

* chore: upgrade GitHub Actions to Node.js 24-compatible versions

- Update actions/checkout v4 -> v6
- Update docker/build-push-action v5 -> v7
- Update docker/login-action v3 -> v4
- Update docker/setup-buildx-action v3 -> v4
- Update docker/metadata-action v5 -> v5.8

Addresses Node.js 20 deprecation warning ahead of June 2026 deadline

* Fixed docker metadata typo

* Upgraded test.yml workflow actions

* Update src/test/setup.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update docker-compose.yml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update .github/workflows/docker.yml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update src/lib/storage.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update src/components/__tests__/navbar.test.tsx

Extend scenario contract for SATCOM/RF and Launch/Transfer expansions

Add new top-level branches to createEmptyScenario() with safe defaults:

  jammingResults, sigintResults, groundStationRecommendations
- Launch/Transfer: launchScenario, launchWindows, launchSolutions,
  rpoPlans, transferPlans, missionLegs, deltaVBudget,
  infrastructureDataRefs

Update validateScenario() with array-type checks for new array fields.
Update getScenarioSummary() to surface counts for new branches.
Migration back-fill works via existing mergeScenarioUpdates() path.
Version stays at '2.0' — these are additive schema extensions.

Add sample data JSON seed files for architecture readiness

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix DownloadToFileAsync partial-file bug and improve presign error reporting

- DownloadToFileAsync: obtain presigned URL before creating the destination
  file so a presign failure never creates an empty file. On any subsequent
  transfer failure or cancellation, delete the partial file via a finally
  block so callers never see a misleading partial/empty destination.
  Both overloads (basic and access-mode) updated consistently.

- IntegratedS3Client.PresignObjectAsync: replace EnsureSuccessStatusCode()
  with a private EnsurePresignSuccessAsync helper that reads the response
  body before throwing, so the HttpRequestException message contains
  actionable server error detail (e.g. ProblemDetails JSON).

- Tests: added IntegratedS3ClientTests.cs covering PresignObjectAsync
  error-reporting (403, 400, 503, empty body). Added four targeted tests
  in IntegratedS3ClientTransferTests: presign-failure does not create
  file, transfer-error deletes partial file, cancellation deletes partial
  file, and access-mode overload transfer-error deletes partial file.
  Extracted NoOpHttpMessageHandler and added FixedStatusHttpMessageHandler
  as class-level helpers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: pre-generate share image on mount for instant share sheet

- Generate canvas image when overlay opens (not on button click)
- navigator.share() now fires immediately in user gesture context
- Fixes mobile Safari gesture expiration causing silent share failure
- Added visible error state with retry button
- Console logging for prod debugging

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: prefix-based transformation cache with inline values (#1544)

Redesign the transformation cache to share intermediate results across
rules with common transformation prefixes (e.g. rules using
t:lowercase,t:urlDecodeUni reuse the t:lowercase result cached by an
earlier rule using just t:lowercase).

Key changes:
- Add transformationPrefixIDs to Rule for backward prefix search
- Cache every intermediate transformation step, not just the final result
- Store cache values inline (not pointers) to avoid heap allocations
- Fix ClearTransformations (t:none) to reset transformationsID

Benchmarked against full CRS v4 ruleset (8 runs, benchstat):
  Allocations: -2% (small) to -19% (30 params)
  Memory:      -2% (small) to -12% (30 params)
  Timing:      -5% (small/large), neutral (medium)
  No regressions on any metric.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

after detecting a new version — no manual action needed.

feat(session): generate session names from git branch and random words (#18)

feat(session): generate session names from random words

Replace opaque timestamp-based names (ai-20260305234735-1234) with
human-readable, memorable names that encode the active tool, current
git context, and a random adjective-noun pair.

New format: ai-<tool>-<adjective>-<noun>-<hex>
Example: ai-claude-quirky-tesla-a7f3

 (Docker-style scientist names) and a random_pair() function
- Rewrite gen_session_name() to accept tool_name and stay a pure
 function; call site in new_session() passes tool.name

- Update Features section to mention human-readable session names
- Add Session Naming subsection (7.1) in doc with format explanation
- Update new_session() API docs to describe auto-generated names
- Examples: ai-claude-quirky-tesla-a7f3, ai-copilot-festive-hawking-c2e1

- Add Contents TOC entry for session naming subsection
